2603.18859.title: RewardFlow: Topology-Aware Reward Propagation on State Graphs for Agentic RL with Large Language Models
2603.18859.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) holds significant promise for enhancing the agentic reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) with external environments. However, the inherent sparsity of terminal rewards hinders fine-grained, state-level optimization. Although process reward modeling offers a promising alternative, training dedicated reward models often entails substantial computational costs and scaling difficulties. To address these challenges, we introduce RewardFlow, a lightweight method for estimating state-level rewards tailored to agentic reasoning tasks. RewardFlow leverages the intrinsic topological structure of states within reasoning trajectories by constructing state graphs. This enables an analysis of state-wise contributions to success, followed by topology-aware graph propagation to quantify contributions and yield objective, state-level rewards. When integrated as dense rewards for RL optimization, RewardFlow substantially outperforms prior RL baselines across four agentic reasoning benchmarks, demonstrating superior performance, robustness, and training efficiency. The implementation of RewardFlow is publicly available at https://github.com/tmlr-group/RewardFlow.
2603.18859.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習（RL）對於提升大型語言模型（LLMs）在外部環境中的代理推理能力具有重要的潛力。<lb/>然而，終端獎勵的內在稀疏性妨礙了細緻的狀態級優化。<lb/>儘管過程獎勵建模提供了一種有前景的替代方案，但訓練專用的獎勵模型往往需要大量的計算成本和擴展困難。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了RewardFlow，一種輕量級的方法，用於估計針對代理推理任務的狀態級獎勵。<lb/>RewardFlow通過構建狀態圖，利用推理軌跡中狀態的內在拓撲結構。<lb/>這使得對成功的狀態貢獻進行分析成為可能，隨後進行拓撲感知的圖形傳播，以量化貢獻並產生客觀的狀態級獎勵。<lb/>當作為RL優化的密集獎勵整合時，RewardFlow在四個代理推理基準上顯著超越了先前的RL基準，顯示出卓越的性能、穩健性和訓練效率。<lb/>RewardFlow的實現已在https://github.com/tmlr-group/RewardFlow公開可用。
2603.18859.author: Xiao Feng et.al.
2603.18859.authors: Xiao Feng, Bo Han, Zhanke Zhou, Jiaqi Fan, Jiangchao Yao, Ka Ho Li, Dahai Yu, Michael Kwok-Po Ng
